title: Initial chunk of manpages  for the buildah project
body: Second chunk of manpages.  I think they're all up to current changes, but several need more/better examples.  I've also included a new common routine that will check to see if a string is a prefix in one or more strings within a slice.  My thinking is we can use that for the delete (or other commands) to do short name checking.  It's currently not as efficient as I'd like, but thought I'd get the general concept approved before trying to make the search faster.  It is not currently being called by any other function.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

You need to rebase your patches and merge them together, then do a force push.
git rebase -i origin
git push twsweeney --force
You meed to update the Makefile to install convert and install these man pages.
Checking with Nalin about the test errors.
LGTM now after tweaking per Nalin's suggestions.
@rhatdan FWIW, Nalin and I were both not able to get the homu test to re-run last week.   i.e. it never would restart for us.
